channel.title: Comments on: Cicerone – Ray Daniels Interview, Ep. 187
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Paul
item.dc:creator: Paul
item.description:
In reply to <a href="https://beeramerica.tv/cicerone-ray-daniels-interview/comment-page-1/#comment-2066">Mark Whiting</a>.
Cicerone does address this. Cold numbs the pallet, that's a fact, so why do you think the folks at Coor's insist on pushing this in all of their advertising? I'm not saying they brew bad beer, it's just a beer you don't want to drink above 40 degrees.
item.content:encoded:
In reply to Mark Whiting.

Cicerone does address this. Cold numbs the pallet, that’s a fact, so why do you think the folks at Coor’s insist on pushing this in all of their advertising? I’m not saying they brew bad beer, it’s just a beer you don’t want to drink above 40 degrees.

item.title: By: Mark Whiting
item.dc:creator: Mark Whiting
item.description:
Thanks for posting this interview, and for the beer clean glass edu at the end.
The other issue I find way too often, is beer served too cold. This is a frequent problem here in Australia where macro beers are all of tasteless lager variety from a hot country. Sure, in the height of summer, working outside, this is perfect. But, when you want an ale, its still served at lager temps. Sorry guys, no idea! Maybe I am just being pedantic.
I am guessing Cicerone addresses correct serving temperature as well?
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for posting this interview, and for the beer clean glass edu at the end.

The other issue I find way too often, is beer served too cold. This is a frequent problem here in Australia where macro beers are all of tasteless lager variety from a hot country. Sure, in the height of summer, working outside, this is perfect. But, when you want an ale, its still served at lager temps. Sorry guys, no idea! Maybe I am just being pedantic.

I am guessing Cicerone addresses correct serving temperature as well?

item.title: By: Mike Dunn
item.dc:creator: Mike Dunn
item.description: Love the series Paul! Looking forward to the next episodes!
item.content:encoded: Love the series Paul! Looking forward to the next episodes!

item.title: By: Paul Leone
item.dc:creator: Paul Leone
item.description:
Hi Andy,
I wouldn't say we are done, but since I (Paul) got my job as the Executive Director of the New York State Brewers Association, I've been really busy getting that position up and running. John is in Savannah and we haven't gotten together much, but we both want to continue this! I have about a dozen unedited episodes I need to dive into and edit and you have just motivated me to do so, so thank you!
item.content:encoded:
Hi Andy,

I wouldn’t say we are done, but since I (Paul) got my job as the Executive Director of the New York State Brewers Association, I’ve been really busy getting that position up and running. John is in Savannah and we haven’t gotten together much, but we both want to continue this! I have about a dozen unedited episodes I need to dive into and edit and you have just motivated me to do so, so thank you!

item.title: By: Andy
item.dc:creator: Andy
item.description: So what's the deal guys? All done posting vids?
item.content:encoded: So what’s the deal guys? All done posting vids?

item.title: By: Chris
item.dc:creator: Chris
item.description:
Damn dude, if you were in DC you and Pinkerton could'a stayed at the house for free. Let me know next time. A buddy of mine lives next to the guy who owns a hops farm (and Frederick Brewing Co.). Maybe I can hook you up. Good interview.
Chris
item.content:encoded:
Damn dude, if you were in DC you and Pinkerton could’a stayed at the house for free. Let me know next time. A buddy of mine lives next to the guy who owns a hops farm (and Frederick Brewing Co.). Maybe I can hook you up. Good interview.

Chris
